title: viola arvensis subsp. henriquesii
intro: Viola arvensis subsp. henriquesii, also known as the Henrique's field pansy, is a subspecies of the common field pansy plant (Viola arvensis) native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Viola arvensis subsp. henriquesii include its ability to reduce anxiety and stress, improve sleep quality, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
